stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • José Martí (1853–1895) was a Cuban writer and political activist.
  • Ismaelillo (1882) and Versos sencillos (1891) are two acclaimed poetry collections that Martí published before settling permanently in New York.
  • Martí relocated to New York City in 1881 but traveled extensively before making it his long-term base in the early 1890s.
  • He is best known for his role in organizing the Cuban independence movement during his New York years.
  • His most famous later writings include the essay collection Nuestra América (1891) and the founding documents of the Cuban Revolutionary Party (1892).
stem: The student wants to emphasize José Martí's accomplishments before settling permanently in New York. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Cuban writer José Martí is the author of works such as Ismaelillo (1882), Versos sencillos (1891), Nuestra América (1891), and the founding documents of the Cuban Revolutionary Party (1892).
In the early 1890s, José Martí settled permanently in New York City, where he would become known for organizing the Cuban independence movement.
Before settling permanently in New York, José Martí — author of the essay collection Nuestra América (1891) and the founding documents of the Cuban Revolutionary Party (1892) — traveled extensively.

Although he is best known as the organizer of the Cuban independence movement, José Martí had already published two acclaimed poetry collections before settling permanently in New York: Ismaelillo (1882) and Versos sencillos (1891).

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. This choice contrasts Martí's fame as the organizer of the Cuban independence movement with his earlier achievements as a poet, naming the two collections he published before settling permanently in New York.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence lists some of Martí's works but does not distinguish those he wrote before settling permanently in New York from those he wrote afterward. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence only mentions when he settled in New York and what he would become known for afterward, not his earlier accomplishments. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence misrepresents the notes: Nuestra América and the founding documents of the Cuban Revolutionary Party are described as his later works, not works published before his settling in New York.
